danger_signals.visual_form: Alien lifeforms and environmental hazards on map
danger_signals.meaning: Natural world becoming increasingly hostile obstacle; threat escalates with game progression

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct creature sprites distinct from human units
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental danger; creature behavior changes based on world state and human interference

danger_signals.visual_form: Faction-colored unit sprites on map grid
danger_signals.meaning: Military threat; visual distinction by faction color/design indicates which faction poses danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Unity spacecraft deterioration in opening narrative
danger_signals.meaning: Cascading societal collapse; catalyst for faction emergence and conflict

safety_signals.visual_form: Map regions with faction-colored borders and city improvements
safety_signals.meaning: Controlled, settled space; lower danger from native life

safety_signals.visual_form: Tech tree progression and facility construction icons
safety_signals.meaning: Increased capability and security; advancing civilization reduces vulnerability

safety_signals.visual_form: Treaty indicators and diplomatic relation displays
safety_signals.meaning: Non-aggression pacts and trade agreements reduce faction conflict risk

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Thriving xenofungus, native life preservation, minimal industrial sprawl
Dense urban centers, minimal resource diversity, militant infrastructure
Tech labs, research centers, experimental installations
Trade routes, commercial bases, resource extraction facilities

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Combat-optimized unit sprites with military bearings

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Diplomatic centers, neutral ground, treaty monuments

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Scattered pod landing sites; minimal faction territory on large unexplored map
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Limited unit movement range; factions avoid direct contact; exploration prioritized

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Mid game - territorial consolidation and ideological conflict
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Expanded faction territories with distinct color borders; increasing unit density; visible infrastructure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Diplomatic tensions visualized through unit positioning; increased military mobilization; tech race indicators

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased xenofungus coverage; rare alien lifeforms appearing; environmental hazards visually prominent
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Native life units become more aggressive; human units suffer environmental attrition; faction control destabilized by planetary forces

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Leader portraits and monologue visuals reflect ideology; unit/building aesthetics match faction philosophy
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Social engineering choices visible through policy icons; environmental policies manifest in xenofungus management; militarism visible in unit production priorities

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Unit and facility designs evolve with tech tree; visual sophistication increases; building aesthetics shift from primitive to advanced
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: New unit types appear; tactical options expand; faction power differential becomes visible through capability display

environmental_storytelling.example: Lady Deirdre Skye (Scottish activist) has green-themed visuals; Chairman Yang (Legalist) has red militaristic presentation; visual characterization reinforces ideological position
Scientific advancement accompanied by faction leader voice-overs that contextualize discovery within faction ideology; reveals character motivation and world philosophy through narration

environmental_storytelling.technique: Map terrain and faction infrastructure placement
environmental_storytelling.example: Gaia's Stepdaughters preserve xenofungus forests visually; Morgan Industries show resource extraction; Spartan Federation displays fortified military positions; playstyle directly manifests in landscape aesthetics

environmental_storytelling.technique: Native alien lifeform behavior and distribution
environmental_storytelling.example: Xenofungus growth patterns reflect Planet's sentience awakening; alien creature encounters become more frequent and aggressive as game progresses; environmental visual threat escalation
Each faction's units and structures have distinct visual style matching ideology: Hive has uniform mechanical aesthetic; Believers have sacred architecture; Morgan has commercial styling
Alliance status, trade routes, and treaty agreements displayed through map overlays and leader encounter visuals; political landscape made readable through UI design

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Minimal presence; low threat; sparse distribution on map
Increased territorial aggression; attacks on human units; expansion across map; visible hostility escalation
Units cluster near bases; fortifications constructed; reduced exploration movement
Dispersed unit placement; rapid base construction; territory claim visible through unit advancement
Positive dialogue tone; visual presentation emphasizes cooperation; trade and alliance proposals
Negative dialogue tone; threatening postures; ultimatum presentations; war declarations preceded by visual confrontation
Voice manifestation increases; environmental phenomena become major gameplay factor; alien intelligence becomes narrative presence

notes: Alpha Centauri uses faction color-coding as primary visual identity system across map, units, and buildings. Leader portraits and voice acting provide character-driven narrative that contextualizes faction ideology. Environmental storytelling heavily relies on terrain modification and infrastructure placement as manifestation of faction philosophy. The game's central mechanic of Planet's growing sentience is communicated through escalating native lifeform threat and environmental hazard visualization. Diplomatic and military postures are readable through unit positioning and leader encounter cinematics. Tech tree advancement visually manifests in unit/building aesthetic evolution.
